action.hypothesis: `lowrank_soft_codebook_residual` has now entered the frontier at K=16 and was already close at K=24, so the next informative test is whether the same soft coarse residual coding can also compete at K=32 and give the family multiple Pareto-frontier wins across the mid-K band.
action.summary: Run a pure parameter comparison for `lowrank_soft_codebook_residual` at `K=32` while keeping the rest of the winning recipe fixed. The family already produced a near-frontier K=24 result and now owns K=16, so the highest-value next step is to map whether its gains persist at the larger covered mid-K anchor.
action.self_review: This keeps a single hypothesis and changes only K, which cleanly tests whether the new family generalizes across the mid-K regime. The main risk is that `lowrank_two_stage_residual` remains stronger at K=32 even if the soft-codebook family helps at tighter sparsity, but the experiment is still high-value because a K=32 win would materially expand the family's frontier coverage.
notes_to_memory: Round 64 tests whether `lowrank_soft_codebook_residual`, now on the frontier at K=16 and near-frontier at K=24, can also compete at K=32.
Keep architecture and recipe fixed (`EF=12`, `signum`, `LR=3.2e-3`, `AUXK_ALPHA=0.0`) so K is the only changing variable.
Success criterion is beating the current K=32 anchor (`lowrank_two_stage_residual`, FVU 0.08931536227464676) or establishing another clearly competitive mid-K point for the family.
next_hypotheses: If `lowrank_soft_codebook_residual` improves the K=32 anchor, test K=8 to see whether the family can cover an even broader low-K frontier band.
If it is close but slightly behind at K=32, consider one confirmatory rerun before deciding whether the family truly tops out around K=16-24.
If it clearly loses at K=32, keep the family as a K=16 specialist and resume searching for a different architecture for the larger mid-K slots.

result.description: Run a pure parameter comparison for `lowrank_soft_codebook_residual` at `K=32` while keeping the rest of the winning recipe fixed. The family already produced a near-frontier K=24 result and now owns K=16, so the highest-value next step is to map whether its gains persist at the larger covered mid-K anchor.

The main risk is that `lowrank_two_stage_residual` remains stronger at K=32 even if the soft-codebook family helps at tighter sparsity, but the experiment is still high-value because a K=32 win would materially expand the family's frontier coverage.
